title: Temporal Workflow Snapshot and Resume
description: Serialize agent state into WorkflowSnapshot, persist via Temporal durable execution, deserialize and resume sandbox session after replay or fork
main_activities: 6.   Runner.run(agent, input, run_config, hooks, previous_response_id) -> RunResult

14.     serialize_resume_state builds: {backend_id, current_agent_key, current_agent_name, session_state: client.serialize_session_state(resources.state), sessions_by_agent: _serialize_sessions_by_agent(client)}
20.   Looks up source SessionInfo from _sessions registry
27.   Extracts portable snapshot = workflow_snapshot.snapshot (SnapshotBase, no backend session_state)

28.   Starts new child workflow: workflow.start_child_workflow(AgentWorkflow.run, AgentRequest(snapshot=snapshot, history=workflow_snapshot.history, previous_response_id=..., sandbox_session_state=None))
33.   No existing resources -> calls _create_resources(agent, capabilities, is_resumed_state)
37.     Looks up sessions_by_agent by resume_key (stable name-based key) or agent_id
38.     Falls back to current_agent_key / current_agent_name matching
40.   IF resumed payload found (turn-to-turn or RunState resume):
42.     _process_resumed_state_manifest(agent, capabilities, session_state) applies capability manifest transforms
47.     client.create(snapshot=snapshot_spec, manifest=effective_manifest, options=options) -> new SandboxSession seeded from snapshot
50. ensure_session calls resources.ensure_started() -> session.start() if not running
51. Returns BaseSandboxSession; agent tools execute within resumed workspace

key_data_structures.WorkflowSnapshot: sandbox_session_state (backend-specific, nullable), snapshot (SnapshotBase portable, nullable), previous_response_id (str|None), history (list[dict])
key_data_structures.AgentRequest: messages, cwd, backend (str), sandbox_session_state, snapshot (SnapshotBase), previous_response_id, history, manifest
key_data_structures.serialize_resume_state output: {backend_id, current_agent_key, current_agent_name, session_state: dict, sessions_by_agent: {key: {agent_name, session_state}}}
key_data_structures.SandboxSessionState: Pydantic model with manifest, parsed via SandboxSessionState.parse(dict)
key_data_structures.SnapshotBase: Portable snapshot; parsed via SnapshotBase.parse(dict); survives backend switches

resume_paths: turn-to-turn: _sandbox_session_state held in AgentWorkflow memory, passed as RunConfig.session_state each turn
RunState resume: serialize_resume_state() -> RunState._sandbox -> _resume_state_payload_for_agent() -> client.deserialize_session_state() -> client.resume()
fork-from-snapshot: WorkflowSnapshot.snapshot passed as RunConfig.snapshot -> client.create(snapshot=...) seeds new session from workspace filesystem
branching_conditions: sandbox_config.session not None -> use live injected session (no create/resume)
resumed payload from run_state -> client.deserialize + resume
cross_backend fork -> session_state dropped, only portable snapshot carried
pause_requested -> _paused=True, waits for next message before proceeding
temporal_mechanisms: @workflow.run: AgentWorkflow.run() is the durable event loop
@workflow.query: get_snapshot(), get_turn_state(), get_history() — read-only state reads
@workflow.update: pause(), switch_backend() — request-reply mutations with await
@activity.defn: pause_workflow, query_workflow_snapshot, switch_workflow_backend — run outside sandbox
SandboxedWorkflowRunner: workflow code runs in restricted sandbox; activities bypass it
parent_close_policy=ABANDON: child AgentWorkflows survive SessionManagerWorkflow restarts
key_relationships: WorkflowSnapshot carries state between AgentWorkflow instances (fork boundary)
SandboxRuntimeSessionManager wraps BaseSandboxClient to create/resume sessions
SnapshotBase (portable) vs SandboxSessionState (backend-specific) — fork uses only SnapshotBase
important_notes: WorkflowSnapshot.snapshot is SnapshotBase (portable workspace filesystem snapshot), not session_state
SandboxSessionState is backend-specific and NOT passed across forks
serialize_resume_state() is called during cleanup() after each Runner.run()
_resume_state_payload_for_agent() uses stable agent name-based keys for multi-agent runs
Temporal durable execution replays workflow history; activities (LLM calls, sandbox ops) are NOT replayed
pause() is an @workflow.update (awaitable) to safely quiesce before snapshot query
All sandbox operations run as Temporal activities via OpenAIAgentsPlugin
